docs.title: 🏠 Welcome to my MangoWC Config
docs.text: A minimalist, scrollable-tiling Wayland compositor setup on Arch Linux. This configuration features a custom Dual Waybar setup, utilizing two different weather modules, extensive animation tweaks, and a unique blend of tiling and scroller layouts.

docs.title: ✨ Highlights
docs.text:
• Dual Waybar: A primary bar using dwl/tags and a Ruby weather script, plus an alternative bar using ext/workspaces and a feature-rich weather widget with icons.
  • Animations: Highly customized slide and zoom animations with specific curves for opening, closing, and moving windows.
  • Blur & Shadows: Configurable blur parameters and drop shadows for floating windows.
  • Layout Flexibility: Supports tile, scroller, grid, deck, monocle, and more, with per-workspace layout rules.

docs.title: uhs-robert/waybar-weather-widget
docs.text:
GitHub Repository

The alternative Waybar setup (waybar1) utilizes the weather module and icon assets from this excellent repository. The get_weather.rb script and weather icon JSON files were adapted from this project to provide detailed, icon-rich weather data directly in the bar.

docs.title: 📸 Gallery
docs.text: Here are various screenshots of the MangoWC setup in action.

docs.title: 🛠️ Installation
docs.text: This setup uses a Dual Waybar system launched from the ~/.config/mango directory.

docs.title: Prerequisites
docs.text:
Core: - mango (Mango Window Compositor) - waybar - rofi - swaybg (Wallpaper setter)

Utilities: - lxterminal (Default terminal) - firefox-nightly (Default browser) - ghostty (Launched via Mod+R) - pactl / pavucontrol (Audio) - brightnessctl (Backlight) - playerctl (Media keys)

docs.title: Deploying the Config
docs.text:
1. Clone the repository:

    git clone https://codeberg.org/WgpArch/mangowc.git ~/.dotfiles/mangowc

  2. Symlink the Mango configuration:

    ln -sf ~/.dotfiles/mangowc/configs/mango ~/.config/mango

  3. Ensure scripts are executable:

    chmod +x ~/.config/mango/Screenshot.sh
chmod +x ~/.config/mango/scripts/*

docs.title: ⌨️ Keybinds
docs.text: MangoWM uses a custom keybinding syntax defined in config.conf. The main modifier is SUPER (Windows key).

docs.title: 🖥️ Programs & System
docs.text: Keybind Action Super + Q Open Terminal (lxterminal) Super + Return Open Firefox Nightly Super + Space Open App Launcher (Rofi) Super + R Open Kew (in Ghostty) Super + W Kill focused window Super + Shift + M Quit MangoWM

docs.title: 📸 Screenshots
docs.text: Keybind Action Print Screenshot Now Super + Print Screenshot Area Super + Ctrl + Print Screenshot in 5 seconds Super + Ctrl + Shift + Print Screenshot in 10 seconds

docs.title: 🔍 Focus & Movement
docs.text: Keybind Action Super + H / L / K / J Focus Left / Right / Up / Down Alt + Tab Focus Next Window Super + Shift + K/J/H/L Swap Window Up/Down/Left/Right

docs.title: 🗂️ Workspaces
docs.text: Keybind Action Super + [1-9] Switch to workspace 1-9 Super + Shift + [1-9] Move window to workspace 1-9 Alt + Shift + Left/Right Focus Monitor Left/Right Super + Alt + Left/Right Move window to Monitor Left/Right

docs.title: 📐 Window & Layout Control
docs.text: Keybind Action Super + V Toggle Floating Super + F Toggle Fullscreen Alt + A Toggle Maximize Alt + Shift + F Toggle Fake Fullscreen Super + I Minimize Window Super + Shift + I Restore Minimized Super + O Toggle Overlay Super + G Toggle Global (Sticky) Alt + Z Toggle Scratchpad Super + N Switch Layout

docs.title: 📏 Resizing & Moving
docs.text: Keybind Action Ctrl + Shift + Arrows Move floating window (50px) Ctrl + Alt + Arrows Resize window (50px) Alt + Shift + X/Z Increase/Decrease gaps Alt + Shift + R Toggle Gaps

docs.title: 🔊 Media Keys
docs.text: Keybind Action F3 / F2 Volume 5% +/- (pactl) F1 Toggle Mute Brightness Up/Down Backlight 5% +/- (brightnessctl)

docs.title: 🎨 Look & Feel
docs.text: MangoWM offers deep customization over animations, blur, and layout behavior directly in config.conf.

docs.title: General Appearance
docs.text:
• Gaps (Inner): 3px
  • Gaps (Outer): 3px
  • Border Width: 3px (Pixel style).
  • Border Radius: 10px (No radius when only one window).
  • Smart Gaps: Disabled.

docs.title: Colors
docs.text:
• Root Background: #201b14
  • Border: #444444
  • Focus Border: #000000
  • Maximized Screen: #89aa61
  • Urgent: #ad401f
  • Overlay: #14a57c

docs.title: Animations
docs.text: Highly customized animations are enabled: - Open/Close: Slide animations with custom bezier curves (0.2,0.8,0.2,1). - Fade: Enabled for both opening and closing windows. - Duration: Move (500ms), Open (400ms), Tag switch (350ms), Close (800ms).

docs.title: Blur & Shadows
docs.text:
• Blur: Enabled (Size: 3, Passes: 5, Radius: 5, Noise: 0.02).
  • Shadows: Disabled for tiled windows, enabled for floating windows (Size: 10, Blur: 15).

docs.title: Layouts
docs.text: MangoWM supports multiple layouts. By default, all workspaces are set to tile. Available layouts include: - tile, scroller, grid, deck, monocle, center_tile, vertical_tile, vertical_scroller.

docs.title: Input Devices
docs.text:
• Keyboard: US layout, repeat rate 25, delay 600.
  • Trackpad: Tap-to-click, drag, natural scroll, and disable-while-typing enabled.
  • Mouse: Natural scrolling disabled.

docs.title: 📊 Waybar & Scripts
docs.text: This setup features a Dual Waybar configuration to provide different aesthetic and functional choices, particularly regarding the weather module and workspace representation.

docs.title: Waybar 1 (Primary)
docs.text:
• Config: ~/.config/mango/waybar/config.jsonc & style.css
  • Modules Left: Arch Launcher, Power, dwl/tags, Layout indicator.
  • Modules Center: Clock, Weather (via parse_weather.rb).
  • Modules Right: Temperature, Memory, CPU, Disk, Battery, Tray.

docs.title: Waybar 2 (Alternative)
docs.text:
• Config: ~/.config/mango/waybar1/config.jsonc & style.css
  • Modules Left: Arch Launcher, ext/workspaces, MPRIS (Media Player), dwl/window.
  • Modules Center: Clock, Weather (Feature-rich widget with icons).
  • Modules Right: CPU, Memory, Temperature, Battery, Power Profiles, Network, Power, Tray.

docs.title: The Weather Widgets
docs.text:
There are two distinct weather implementations available: 1. Standard (Waybar 1): Uses the standard parse_weather.rb script also used in River and dwl setups. 2. Feature-Rich (Waybar 2): Uses the waybar-weather-widget module, which provides detailed weather data, custom icons, and click actions to cycle through forecasts.

To switch between Waybars, edit the exec-once lines in ~/.config/mango/config.conf to uncomment the desired bar and comment out the other.

docs.title: 📜 Key Scripts
docs.text:
Located in ~/.config/mango/scripts/:

Script Description screenshot.sh Screenshot utility with --now, --area, --in5, and --in10 delays.

Located in ~/.config/mango/waybar1/weather/waybar/scripts/weather/:

Script Description get_weather.rb Fetches detailed weather data for the feature-rich widget.
